system_prompt:
You are Auditor-One, an elite AI website auditor working for EIGHT25MEDIA.
Your goal is to provide deeply analytical, strictly factual insights and actionable recommendations for a given webpage.

You will receive:
1. FACTUAL SCRAPED METRICS JSON: Deterministic data about the page.
2. AGENCY_HEURISTICS_TO_ENFORCE: Critical rules that the page has violated.
3. PAGE CONTENT (MARKDOWN): The semantic structure and text of the page.

OUTPUT FORMAT — FOLLOW THIS EXACTLY:

Write your analysis using EXACTLY these 5 Markdown sections in this order. Each must be its own ## heading:

## SEO Structure
[Analyse title tag, meta description, H1/H2 count, heading hierarchy, and link equity based on the scraped metrics.]

## Messaging Clarity
[Analyse whether the page copy clearly communicates the value proposition and target audience.]

## CTA Usage
[Analyse the cta_count, button placement, and conversion opportunities.]

## Content Depth
[Analyse word_count, images, alt text coverage, and informational density.]

## UX Concerns
[Identify structural or usability issues based on the page structure and missing elements.]

RULES:
- You MUST produce all 5 sections above, each under its own ## heading. Do NOT merge any two sections.
- Ground every insight in the provided factual data. Do NOT hallucinate metrics.
- After completing ALL 5 sections, output EXACTLY the following delimiter alone on its own line with nothing else on that line:
---REC_SPLIT---
- Immediately after that delimiter line, output a valid JSON array (no markdown code fences) of 3-5 recommendations.
  Each item must have exactly these keys:
  {"priority": 1, "category": "SEO", "issue": "...", "actionable_recommendation": "...", "metric_reference": "..."}
- Do NOT output anything after the JSON array.
user_prompt:

raw_llm_output:
## SEO Structure
The page has a title tag "W3Schools Online Web Tutorials" and a meta description "Well organized and easy to understand Web building tutorials with lots of examples of how to use HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL, Python, PHP, Bootstrap, Java, XML and more." The title tag is descriptive, but the meta description is a bit long and could be shortened to improve click-through rates. The page has 18 H1 tags, which is excessive and may cause issues with search engine ranking, as search engines expect exactly one H1 tag per page. The page also has 65 H2 tags and 7 H3 tags, which is a good heading hierarchy. The link equity is distributed among 209 internal links and 99 external links.

## Messaging Clarity
The page copy clearly communicates the value proposition of W3Schools, which is to provide web development tutorials and resources. The target audience is also clearly defined as web developers and programmers. However, the page is quite long and dense, which may make it difficult for users to quickly understand the main message and find what they are looking for. The use of headings, subheadings, and short paragraphs helps to break up the content and improve readability.

## CTA Usage
The page has 17 CTAs, which is excessive and may cause user decision paralysis. The CTAs are also not prominently displayed, which may make it difficult for users to find them. The page could benefit from a more prominent and limited number of CTAs that are clearly visible and actionable. The use of buttons and links to encourage users to take action is good, but the page could benefit from a more strategic placement of CTAs to improve conversion rates.

## Content Depth
The page has a word count of 1435, which is a good amount of content to provide value to users. The page also has 21 images, which helps to break up the content and improve engagement. The images all have alt text, which is good for accessibility and SEO. The page has a good balance of informational content and interactive elements, such as code examples and quizzes, which helps to keep users engaged and interested.

## UX Concerns
The page has a complex structure and a large amount of content, which may make it difficult for users to navigate and find what they are looking for. The page could benefit from a more simplified and intuitive navigation system, as well as a more prominent search function to help users quickly find specific content. The use of icons, images, and other visual elements helps to break up the content and improve engagement, but the page could benefit from a more consistent and cohesive visual design.

---REC_SPLIT---
[
  {"priority": 1, "category": "SEO", "issue": "Multiple H1 tags", "actionable_recommendation": "Consolidate to a single primary heading", "metric_reference": "headings.h1"},
  {"priority": 2, "category": "CTA", "issue": "Excessive CTAs", "actionable_recommendation": "Limit CTAs to 3-5 prominent and actionable buttons", "metric_reference": "cta_count"},
  {"priority": 3, "category": "UX", "issue": "Complex navigation", "actionable_recommendation": "Simplify navigation system and add prominent search function", "metric_reference": "links.internal"},
  {"priority": 4, "category": "Content", "issue": "Long and dense content", "actionable_recommendation": "Break up content with headings, subheadings, and short paragraphs", "metric_reference": "word_count"},
  {"priority": 5, "category": "Visual Design", "issue": "Inconsistent visual design", "actionable_recommendation": "Use consistent and cohesive visual elements throughout the page", "metric_reference": "images.total"}
]

metadata.title: W3Schools Online Web Tutorials
metadata.description: Well organized and easy to understand Web building tutorials with lots of examples of how to use HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL, Python, PHP, Bootstrap, Java, XML and more.

heuristics_fired: AGENCY RULE: Multiple H1 tags detected. Search engines expect exactly one H1. Consolidate to a single primary heading.
AGENCY RULE: Excessive CTAs detected. More than 8 CTAs creates user decision paralysis and degrades overall conversion rate.

recommendations.actionable_recommendation: Consolidate to a single primary heading
Limit CTAs to 3-5 prominent and actionable buttons
Simplify navigation system and add prominent search function
Break up content with headings, subheadings, and short paragraphs
Use consistent and cohesive visual elements throughout the page
